person_name: Theophilus (Ammianus, accused of treason, 4th century Roman official)
person_name_full: Theophilus (Ammianus, accused of treason, 4th century Roman official)
notes: 4th century Roman official, governor of Syria, mentioned by Ammianus Marcellinus and others, accused of treason.

docs.title: BOOK XIV
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … had fallen upon Theophilus who was innocent; and, secondly, that Serenianus who deserved universal execration, was acquitted without the general feeling being able to offer any effectual remonstrance. 9.

docs.title: Letter LXIII
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: … persons are displeased that you are so long-suffering in regard to that shocking heresy, and that you suppose yourself able by such lenity to amend those who are attacking the Church's vitals.

docs.title: Letter LXXXVI
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: I grieved to find you too forbearing, and, knowing nothing of the course shaped by the pilot, I yearned for the destruction of those abandoned men. But, as I now see, you have had your hand raised and, if you have delayed to strike, it has only been that you might strike harder.
